title: ParseArtifact
description: Versioned parser output consumed by downstream Solid Stats services.

aggregates.description: Derived aggregate projections and auditable contribution references.

contract_version.description: Parser contract version used to shape this artifact.

diagnostics.description: Structured warnings and errors that did not necessarily fail parsing.

entities.description: Observed entity and identity facts from the replay.

extensions.description: Stable extension object reserved for forward-compatible parser metadata.

failure.description: Structured failure details required when status is `failed`.

produced_at.description: Optional production timestamp supplied by non-deterministic adapters.

replay.description: Normalized replay metadata when it could be extracted.

source.description: Replay source identity and checksum metadata.

AggregateContributionKind.description: Kind of aggregate contribution represented in the artifact.

oneOf.description: Contribution to a legacy-compatible counter projection.
Contribution that can be consumed as a bounty calculation input.
Contribution that can be consumed as a vehicle score input.
Contribution to killed/killer or teamkilled/teamkiller relationship summaries.
Contribution kind could not be classified while preserving source evidence.

AggregateContributionRef.description: Auditable link between a derived aggregate value and its normalized source evidence.

contribution_id.description: Stable contribution identifier within the artifact.

event_id.description: Normalized event identifier when this contribution derives from a single event.

source_refs.description: Non-empty set of source references backing the contribution.

value.description: Contribution payload, shaped by the rule and projection.

AggregateSection.description: Aggregate projection section of a successful or partial parse artifact.

contributions.description: Auditable contribution list used to derive projections.

projections.description: Stable key-value projections for legacy and parser-owned aggregate surfaces.

oneOf.description: SHA-256 checksum.

Confidence.description: Confidence score for inferred field values.

Diagnostic.description: Structured warning or error emitted during parsing.

expected_shape.description: Expected source shape, when the diagnostic concerns schema drift.

json_path.description: JSON path to the replay source field, when available.

observed_shape.description: Observed source shape, when the diagnostic concerns schema drift.

parser_action.description: Parser action taken in response to the issue.

DiagnosticSeverity.description: Severity of a structured parser diagnostic.

oneOf.description: Informational diagnostic.
Warning diagnostic that did not necessarily fail parsing.
Error diagnostic for failed or partially failed parsing.

EntityCompatibilityHint.description: Auditable legacy compatibility hint for observed entity projection behavior.

kind.description: Compatibility behavior represented by this hint.

observed_name.description: Observed name involved in this compatibility behavior.

related_entity_ids.description: Related source entity identifiers that participate in this hint.

rule_id.description: Stable rule identifier that produced this hint.

EntityCompatibilityHintKind.description: Legacy compatibility hint kind attached to an observed entity.

oneOf.description: Entity identity was backfilled from a connected-player event.
Entity is a duplicate-slot same-name candidate for later aggregate projection.
Infantry or player-like unit.
Vehicle entity.
Static weapon entity.
Entity kind could not be classified.
East side.
West side.
Guerilla or independent side.
Civilian side.
Side could not be determined.

EventActorRef.description: Actor identity fragment referenced by a normalized event.

source_entity_id.description: Source entity identifier for the actor.

FieldPresence.description: Explicit state wrapper for fields that may be present, null, unknown, inferred, or irrelevant.

oneOf.description: Field was present in the source or adapter input.

source.description: Source reference for the value, when available.

oneOf.description: Field was explicitly null in the source or domain event.

source.description: Source reference for the null state, when available.

oneOf.description: Field value is unknown.

source.description: Source reference for the unknown state, when available.

oneOf.description: Field value was inferred rather than directly observed.

confidence.description: Optional confidence score for the inference.

source.description: Source reference for the inference, when available.

oneOf.description: Field does not apply to this replay, entity, event, or projection.

reason.description: Reason the field is not applicable.

NormalizedEvent.description: Normalized event with source references and rule provenance.

event_id.description: Stable event identifier within the artifact.

source_refs.description: Non-empty source references backing this event.

oneOf.description: Player or entity connected.
Player or entity disconnected.
Kill event.
Death event.
Teamkill event.
Suicide event.
Player-killed event.
Vehicle-killed event.
Event shape was preserved but could not be classified.

NullReason.description: Reason for an explicit null field state.

oneOf.description: Killer is explicitly null.
Source field value was null.
Source field was empty and normalized to null.

ObservedEntity.description: Observed replay entity with identity facts and source references.

compatibility_hints.description: Legacy compatibility hints without collapsing raw observed entities.

identity.description: Observed identity fields for the entity.

is_player.description: Observed player flag from replay data.

observed_class.description: Observed entity class from replay data.

observed_name.description: Observed entity name from replay data.

source_entity_id.description: Source entity identifier from OCAP data.

ObservedIdentity.description: Observed identity fields preserved without canonical player matching.

job_id.description: Parse job identifier, when supplied by the adapter.

replay_id.description: Replay identifier, when supplied by the caller or source.

source_cause.description: Original lower-level cause, when safe to expose.

source_refs.description: Source references associated with the failure.

stage.description: Parser stage where the failure occurred.

ParseStage.description: Parser stage where a structured failure occurred.

oneOf.description: Input file or object access stage.
Source checksum validation stage.
JSON decoding stage.
Contract or source schema validation stage.
Replay normalization stage.
Aggregate derivation stage.
Output writing or publishing stage.
Internal invariant or unexpected parser stage.

ParseStatus.description: High-level parse outcome recorded in a parser artifact.

oneOf.description: The parser completed without semantic data loss.
The parser completed with warnings or recoverable gaps.
The parser intentionally skipped this replay.
The parser failed before producing a usable artifact.

git_commit.description: Git commit used to build the parser.

ParserInfo.description: Parser binary metadata embedded in each artifact.

build.description: Optional build metadata for non-deterministic adapter contexts.

ReplayMetadata.description: Normalized replay-level metadata from observed OCAP top-level fields.

players_count.description: Observed player count timeline or values.

ReplaySource.description: Replay source identity and checksum metadata.

replay_id.description: Replay identifier when the caller or source provides one.

source_file.description: Source file path or object key.

Retryability.description: Retryability classification for a parse failure.

oneOf.description: Retrying the same job may succeed.
Retrying the same job is not expected to succeed.
Retryability could not be determined.

SourceRef.description: Source coordinate for replay evidence used by normalized events and aggregates.

UnknownReason.description: Reason for an unknown field state.

oneOf.description: `SteamID` is absent from the source.
Winner is absent from the source.
Commander data is absent from the source.
Source field is absent.
Source schema drift prevents a reliable value.
Checksum could not be calculated or supplied.
